title: The Coin in the Fish's Mouth
subtitle: At Capernaum the temple tax is demanded of the Lord, and a stater is drawn from the deep

stillness: Be still, and remember the death of Christ.
so long as you are in the turning of this present life,
let your heart hold these words.
gospel: After this, Jesus walked in Galilee,
in the land where He was conceived and nourished.
And as they were abiding there, Jesus said to them:
Lay up these words in your hearts:
The Son of Man is to be betrayed into the hands of men,
and on the third day He will rise again.
But they did not understand the word,
and it was veiled before them, that they should not perceive it;
and they were afraid to ask Him.
And when they had come to Capernaum,
those who collected the didrachma came to Peter and said:
Does your Master not pay the tax?
Go to the sea, He told him, and cast a hook;
and take the first fish that comes up,
and opening its mouth you will find a stater:
take it, and give it to them for Me and for you.
behold: They are abiding in Galilee, the rolling land,
and again He speaks to them of His Passion,
so that, grown used to the business of His death,
they may be less scandalized when it comes.
The word is veiled before them;
and this, says Bede, came not so much from dullness of mind
as from the loving piety of the Saviour —
they could not believe that He whom they knew to be true God could die.
Now look to Capernaum, the chief city,
where the tax-gatherers of Caesar lodge.
They do not dare to approach the Lord Himself,
for the greatness of His signs;
so they come to Peter alone.
And the Lord, knowing what was said,
sends Peter to the sea to cast a hook,
and to lift from the water the first fish that rises,
and from its mouth to take a stater —
the price for the Lord and for himself.
consider: Consider what was the cause of His betrayal:
by the Father, through exceeding love;
by Himself, through willing obedience;
by the devil, through wicked suggestion;
by Judas, through utmost greed;
by the Jews, through cunning envy;
by Pilate, through unworthy faintheartedness.
Great is the iniquity, that men should kill the Man who is their Saviour!
Yet lest sadness swallow them, He adds: on the third day He will rise.
Marvel at His divinity, laid bare:
for He foretold things to come with certainty,
and at His word a stater was at that moment created in the mouth of the fish.
Spiritually, the fish is Christ, the sea is the world,
the hook is death,
the coin found in His mouth is the price of our redemption,
foretold by the mouth of Christ; and so the tribute was paid, and we were set free.
He owed nothing — as Son of the King by both natures, He was free,
yet He bound Himself to the lesser, paying what He did not owe,
lest He scandalize the collectors,
teaching us to be humble, and to give no cause of stumbling.
And to him who turns in penance it is said:
Go to the sea — to the bitterness of sin in its remembrance;
cast the hook — in the compunction of contrition;
and with mouth opened in confession, give the coin you have found, in satisfaction;
and so the sinner is set free from the tribute of the devil, that exacting collector.
prayer: grant me to go to the sea,
calling my sins to mind in the bitterness of my soul;
and to cast the hook, in the contrition of my heart;
and with mouth opened, in confession,
to pay the coin, in satisfaction;
and so to be freed from the tribute of the devil and his exaction.
Grant me also, turned from my pride,
to become as a little one, lowly in my own eyes;
and so, through the narrow way and the strait gate,
may I be found worthy to enter into the kingdom of blessedness.
And the little ones too, and the lowly, may I receive for Your name's sake,
showing them the kindnesses of charity,
so that, receiving You in them,
I may be received by You in heaven. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Quid primum miror hic nescio, an præsentiam stateris in ore piscis, an magnitudinem virtutis, si ad verbum ejus mox stater in ore piscis creatus est!
latinJewel.english: What I marvel at first here I do not know — whether the presence of the stater in the fish's mouth, or the greatness of the power, if at His word a stater was at that moment created in the fish's mouth!
